page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r9a.large is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the ecs.r9a family. It is powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9T25 processor operating at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance features 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs, utilizing a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. It is provisioned with 16.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per physical core.

This instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and does not include any bundled local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for operating system and data hosting. Network connectivity is configured with a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The instance does not feature dedicated graphics processing units (GPUs) or hardware accelerators, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The ecs.r9a.large provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8, making it a resource-dense option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high compute core counts. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring moderate memory footprints without the overhead of additional vCPUs. Suitable workloads include small-scale databases, in-memory caching, lightweight enterprise applications, and general-purpose tasks requiring dedicated CPU allocation with a larger memory allocation.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r9a.large is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9T25 processor running at 2.7 GHz. Configured with 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs, it provides 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 makes this instance qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive applications that require dedicated CPU resources but do not demand high compute density. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without resource contention, though the lack of local storage requires planning for disk-heavy operations. It is suited for small-scale databases, in-memory caching, and lightweight enterprise applications.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r9a.large is a memory-optimized KVM instance featuring a 2.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9T25 processor, 2 dedicated vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC instance featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for memory-intensive workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9T25 processor running at 2.7 GHz
2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 (8.0 GB per core)
KVM hypervisor with 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for small-scale databases and in-memory caching
